figures.caption: Figure 1: Stage 1 pipeline overview. Top row (A): The input pipeline feeds 50 Kinetics-mini videos through the frozen V-JEPA 2 ViT-L encoder (∇θ = 0), producing B × 1568 × 1024 latent token vectors that are precomputed once and cached. Middle row (A): The Stage A quantizer trains for 3,000 steps on the precomputed vectors using a linear projection layer (1024 → 256 dimensions, followed by LayerNorm and L2 normalization), an EMA-updated codebook (K = 8, γ = 0.90), and a dead-code reset mechanism (β = 2.0 commitment loss); the V-JEPA 2 encoder weights remain fully frozen throughout. Bottom row (B/C): After training, the pipeline is evaluated on two diagnostic criteria: H1 symbol stability (20 repeated forward passes per video; consistency = 100%) and H2 category-contrast experiments (three physical-variable pairs assessed via χ2, MI, and JSD), with a Gaussian-noise random baseline confirming MI ≈ 0 for unstructured inputs. All four pass criteria are satisfied, producing the Stage 1 report and AIM dictionary that certify readiness for Stage 2.
